author: @kokeskokeskokes
text: You are my favourite living scientist. Many times when I search for something interesting I find your stuff. But this one I am little reserved about. Did you test it in the sea for longer periods? How was the algal growth? Didn't the generator diminish its output to nothing over time? Anyway, excellent video as ever.

author: @VenkateshksDr
text:
sorry about that exclamation.  Thought would sound like something out of 19th century science fiction.  The idea is worth looking at though and i will try to get something done on those lines.  I live in India, South and it seems a shame to let all that sunlight and thermal energy go waste.  Got this idea of combining solar water heater and generator.  will tell when i get it to do something.
construction is simple.  Plastic pipes will do and a wooden framework.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: +Venkatesh k.s. i am a huge fan of 19th century science - i kind of think that's what we are doing here anyway lol

author: @VenkateshksDr
text:
Pipes Mr.Robert, Pipes.  I think it can be worked out, to have internal walls of pipes coated with graphene and copper or aluminium rings placed at intervals with small wires to outside surface for contact,  and since any water in pipelines has dissolved minerals, there you go.  
Well, sort of science fiction idea at the moment, but then, today just an idea and tomorrow.............:)

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: +Venkatesh k.s. lol - very true and good idea mate

author: @ScottSextonandDavidLewis
text: You are SO prolific Robert. I haven't time to keep up with my day and night jobs and be able to watch your fascinating works, let alone, YET, do similar experiments with your ink. I will watch eagerly however when your clever idea to dispense with ocean fouling via a plunger system. Later renaissance man.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: lol - i only sleep 4 hours a night mate but i have such alot to do it is almost impossible to get through it all - ah well we try lol

author: @jesslessthemess
text: I love all these ideas for automobile use... you have waste heat, movement, braking.  I can see a huge tank of sloshing water and some graphene plates in the trunk right now :)

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: well a petrol tank is just a huge tank of sloshing fluid - this has the benefit of not being as explosive lol

author: @jasoncunninghambluejmc
text: Your videos are great, you are cutting edge, awesome keep it up. I may have responded to the wrong video but I think you know I met to put it on the vid where you get voltage from ambient air.  The best. These guys use graphene on the "receivers" Check out their channel! Again AWESOME. IonPowerGroup com  White Board  The standard air antenna is I beleive 60ft. off the ground and 120 long in the air but IonGroup has a square in the air.!? ... but only one 120 ft. length is necessary to get 120v. ac I believe without grapene "collectors" Check out inventor 3s' channel on air antennas.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I know the Ion power group - i wrote to them and they didn't bother to reply - so I am sulking at them lol

author: @randomlettersqzkebkw
text:
Very excited to see the upcoming videos.
Its too bad I live far from the sea.
I might look more into your other Thermal Forever Battery video
Really cant wait to see this next video though

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: you don't need to live by the sea for the version I am showing - one friend was suggesting using wind power for a bellows kind of arrangement to 'pump' the water in the tank - but go for it on the forever battery

author: @ArkiverUnifiedEnergy
text: Could the SWAG in the isolated tank generate a voltage with only one conductor in reference to ground? I'm thinking of a possible radiant energy absorption without motion of the salt water.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I haven't tried but that is a very interesting idea - I will try it

author: @robinlawrie7189
text: If you could find a surface that will accept the ink nicely, but allow it to be peeled off after, or alternatively an ink with these properties, you could skip the expensive ohp backing sheet.  Important when the application might need hundreds of sheets.. also be good to find a cheaper salt than copper chloride that has similar performance,  for larger scale applications..

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: all very true - go for it mate

replies.author: @robinlawrie7189
replies.text: Robert Murray-Smith i think i shall :)

author: @robroyan1010
text:
I like it.... I don't know enough about fluid dynamics, but wouldn't a couple of floation devices attached and just allow the device to be thrown around by the wave action be enough, rather then worry about internal moving parts.  I like the KISS principle where possible.

Have you done any bench testing with the device sitting on a seesaw?  It would be interesting to see how multiple sheets work together before you get to far down the enclosure design.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: i Like simple too - but the idea of it fouling is what prompted this and with direct sea contact this would be inevitable - so a bit  of a trade off for lifetime versus complexity

replies.author: @robroyan1010
replies.text: Robert Murray-Smith Yup ... still an enclosed box, but a floating box with internal liquid.  With floatation devices placed to make it tippy.  Or, are you envisioning something sitting on the shore with the waves activating a plunger?  I guess you could also get a plunger affect from an anchor chain.  So many options, I'm interested to see where you go with this.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text:
Rob Royan
I'm thinking shore based - but you are right there are so many places to go with it

author: @Outsideofthebox13
text: Man, this is cool Robert, closer and closer!

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: i'm quite excited about it actually mate - if i didn't have so much else to do I would concentrate on this
